canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Places365 is consistently characterized as a large-scale, scene-centric dataset and benchmark for image classification. Its primary application is for scene recognition and classification tasks, with one definition specifying it is "used to evaluate descriptions on scene categories." The dataset is noted to contain 365 scene categories. In practice, researchers report using Places365 in their experimental setups, including for training models and conducting comprehensive evaluations of their approaches. For instance, one paper observes that a specific model "performs better on the Places365 dataset which is a scene classification dataset."
papers: Bootstrapping Variational Information Pursuit with Large Language and Vision Models for Interpretable Image Classification
Faithful Vision-Language Interpretation via Concept Bottleneck Models
Follow-Up Differential Descriptions: Language Models Resolve Ambiguities for Image Classification

definitions.text: Places365, a scene classification benchmark used to evaluate descriptions on scene categories.
definitions.paper: Bootstrapping Variational Information Pursuit with Large Language and Vision Models for Interpretable Image Classification
